concepts.title: Tropics and Temperate Zones
concepts.description: The Earth is divided into five climate zones: Tropical, Subtropical, Temperate, Polar, and Alpine. The Tropics lie between 23.5° N and S latitudes, and the Temperate zones lie between 23.5° and 66.5° N and S latitudes.

concepts.title: Climate Change Effects
concepts.description: Rising global temperatures lead to melting of polar ice caps, resulting in sea-level rise and increased frequency of extreme weather events such as heatwaves, droughts, and heavy rainfall.

concepts.title: Types of Climate
concepts.description: There are two main types of climate: Maritime (influenced by the ocean) and Continental (influenced by the land). Maritime climates are generally milder, while Continental climates are more extreme.

concepts.title: Köppen Climate Classification
concepts.description: Use the sentence 'Köppen's Climate Classification is Amazingly Simple' to remember the main climate types: A (Tropical), B (Dry), C (Temperate), D (Continental), E (Polar).

concepts.title: Climate Change vs. Global Warming
concepts.description: Do not confuse climate change with global warming. Global warming refers to the increase in average global temperature, while climate change encompasses all changes in climate patterns, including temperature, precipitation, and extreme weather events.

concepts.title: Climate Classification Units
concepts.description: Remember the following units for climate classification: A (Tropical), B (Dry), C (Temperate), D (Continental), E (Polar), H (Mountainous), K (Polar Tundra)

concepts.title: Greenhouse Effect
concepts.description: The greenhouse effect is the process by which certain gases in the Earth's atmosphere, such as carbon dioxide and methane, trap heat from the sun, leading to a warming of the planet.

concepts.title: Causes of Climate Change
concepts.description: Human activities such as burning fossil fuels, deforestation, and industrial processes release greenhouse gases, leading to climate change. Natural factors such as volcanic eruptions and changes in Earth's orbit also contribute to climate change.
